title: keyfile: key seed Vec not zeroized after decrypt
description: decrypt_keyfile() stores the 64-byte raw key seed in a heap Vec<u8> (plaintext variable). When the function returns Ok or Err the Vec is dropped without zeroing, leaving key material in heap memory until the allocator reclaims it. An attacker with read access to process memory (core dump, swap file, cold-boot) can recover the Ed25519 and HPKE seeds. Fix: use a zeroize-on-drop wrapper (e.g. zeroize::Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> or [u8; 64] with explicit ZeroizeOnDrop) for the plaintext buffer in keyfile.rs decrypt_keyfile.
